data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Minimizing choices to improve user response time and decision making.
data.technical_spec: Streamlined menu options and manageable content layouts.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Designing interfaces that allow for quick access to regularly used functions.
data.technical_spec: Larger clickable areas for frequently used buttons or links.

data.title: Law of Common Region
data.description: Elements tend to be perceived as a group if they are sharing an area with a clearly defined boundary.
data.technical_spec: Usage of bordered, boxed sections to group related functions together.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other websites, so design familiarity aids in ease of use.
data.technical_spec: Adherence to conventional navigation patterns and layouts.

data.title: Peak-End Rule
data.description: User experience is defined by the most intense moments and the end of the interaction.
data.technical_spec: Efforts to ensure engaging content display and satisfying end-screen experiences.
